Detailed plan 2026-05-17 broad dogfood/review/optimization pass:
1. Baseline and duplicate check: reuse pm-rnpb as parent after context/search/list-open/list-in-progress; inspect current open/in-progress children before creating anything new.
2. Parallel sub-agent review: use bounded read-only agents for performance/code quality, SDK/package extensibility/schema UX, search/calendar dogfood correctness, and GitHub/CI/privacy/observability findings. Main agent owns mutations and final integration.
3. Manual temp-project dogfood: run the reusable isolated dogfood harness plus additional manual probes for create/update strictness, low-token list/get/search/context outputs, package install/catalog/doctor, calendar create/update/list, semantic/hybrid reindex, linked tests, validate, and health.
4. Performance pass: benchmark common commands with telemetry on/off, inspect startup/import hotspots, slow file paths, repeated tracker loads, CI cache/workflow opportunities, and open performance items pm-gt82/pm-tzwy/pm-1pah/pm-iv1u/pm-27yz.
5. UX/extensibility pass: review contracts/help/SDK/package APIs for excessive schema strictness, duplicate command surfaces, config-driven type/schema behavior, extension capabilities, public SDK exports, and agent-friendly retry guidance.
6. Search/calendar pass: verify qwen/Ollama semantic search and auto-index behavior, hybrid reindex progress/JSON summaries, reminder/event corpus coverage, calendar markdown/json output, and agent-optimized filtering.
7. Observability/security pass: run secret scans and tracked/pm-history targeted checks without committing private ops details; inspect live telemetry/Sentry via existing release gates and ignored scripts under scripts/prod/telemetry only.
8. GitHub/CI/CD pass: inspect issues/PRs/checks/notifications with gh, evaluate auto-release and free-tier workflow efficiency, and integrate actionable findings into existing pm items.
9. Implement scoped fixes only where evidence shows a safe high-value issue; link files/tests/docs to existing items and add findings as comments/learnings instead of duplicate items.
10. Verification and delivery: run focused tests, dogfood, static quality, secret scan, release gate with telemetry/Sentry, coverage if code changes warrant it, commit and push, then wait for GitHub Actions to finish green and record evidence in pm.

value.author: codex-gpt5-audit
value.text: Evidence 2026-05-20 continuation: sub-agent lanes and manual dogfood found actionable issues in dependency security, package catalog verbosity/path exposure, malformed legacy metadata crashes, search projection typos, and optional package command recovery. Fixed brace-expansion override/lockfile to 5.0.6, added null-safe status/runtime normalization plus search scoring for malformed metadata, made search --fields reject unknown selectors, added compact package catalog/list --fields with path-redacted roots, removed package_root from catalog output, and made optional package contract failures suggest pm install <package> --project instead of a bogus --project retry. Verification passed: focused 5-file suite 105 tests, pnpm build, pnpm audit --prod --audit-level moderate, node scripts/check-secrets.mjs, normal dogfood 84 workflows, semantic dogfood 86 workflows with qwen3-embedding:0.6b, and full release gate with 95 files / 1379 tests at 100% coverage plus Sentry high=2 under threshold and telemetry finish_error_rate_pct=2.52. Residuals kept in existing lineages: timezone-aware calendar bucketing, vector prune of deleted IDs, runtime schema-derived strict action schemas, SDK cwd threading, release parallelization, coverage allowlist policy, and large-file split pm-mbdu.

value.author: codex-gpt5-audit
value.text: Evidence 2026-05-20 final dogfood/audit slice: reused existing lineage after pm context/search/list-open/list-in-progress duplicate check. Sub-agent lanes covered SDK/package extensibility, semantic search/calendar, CI/coverage/performance/code quality, telemetry/Sentry/GitHub/privacy, and manual temp-project dogfood UX. Implemented fixes: hardened malformed/non-string status handling behind PM-CLI-R, hardened search scoring for malformed status fields behind PM-CLI-S, rejected unknown search --fields values instead of returning null projections, added compact package catalog/list --fields output without local package_root exposure, fixed optional-package contract recovery to suggest pm install <package> --project, added command-aware allowed-value retry guidance, and updated the production brace-expansion override to >=5.0.6. Verification passed: pnpm build; focused unit suite tests/unit/search-command.spec.ts tests/unit/status-normalization.spec.ts tests/unit/extension-command.spec.ts tests/unit/contracts-command.spec.ts tests/unit/error-guidance.spec.ts (105 tests); pnpm audit --prod --audit-level moderate; node scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs (84 workflows); PM_DOGFOOD_SEMANTIC=1 node scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs (86 workflows, qwen3-embedding:0.6b); node scripts/check-secrets.mjs; node scripts/release/run-gates.mjs --telemetry-mode required --max-sentry-critical 10 --max-sentry-high 20 --max-telemetry-error-rate 10 --json (build, typecheck, docs-skills, static quality, 100% coverage, version policy, secret scan, npx smoke, package dogfood, pack dry-run, compatibility, Sentry/telemetry gate all ok). Residual findings intentionally left in existing lineages rather than duplicated: calendar timezone day bucketing for offset events, semantic full reindex orphan-vector pruning, runtime-customized strict action schema, SDK getContracts cwd behavior, CI workflow serialization/static thresholds, package scaffold SDK imports, and known history-drift/privacy backlog under pm-xk39.

value.author: codex-manual-dogfood
value.text: Manual dogfood findings 2026-05-20: isolated temp project at /tmp/pm-manual-dogfood-20260520-o4D9ev with PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH separated. Covered init --with-packages, create/claim/update/close/release, files/docs/tests/comments/notes/learnings, list/search/context/activity/history, deps lookup, calendar, package catalog/explore/manage/doctor/install, contracts/help, todos/beads import-export surfaces. Key findings: 53 MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warnings from bundled extensions after package install; pm comments on long-lived item returns full history; activity default still prints full patches; dependencies command expectation fails because actual command is deps; calendar event create phrase is silently treated as agenda; calendar same-day from/to rejected; package explore has no query/target filter; todos export silently ignores unsupported --format/--output and writes default folder; package/catalog invalid fields guidance is helpful; history --compact works and is much smaller; linked test sandboxing works with node scripts/run-tests.mjs form.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Sub-agent finding (search/calendar/vector): semantic reindex can use extension embed providers but search requires full query providers; gc embeddings leaves vectorization-status/lancedb state; auto-index refresh warnings are profile-only; completions advertise optional package commands; LM Studio works through openai.base_url but is not discoverable. Implementing safe cleanup fix now; larger provider/completion UX gaps remain tracked under pm-rnpb follow-ups.

value.author: codex-gpt5

Keeping this branch to compact history + semantic gc cleanup; performance/test-sharding/static-quality ratchets remain in existing pm-gt82/pm-mbdu/pm-rnpb follow-ups.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Startup perf: the single biggest CLI startup cost was importing zod (~85ms) eagerly via settings.ts, which is on the hot read path of nearly every command (list/get/search/context/create/update all readSettings). zod was used in exactly ONE file. Replacing it with a dependency-free validator (src/core/store/settings-validator.ts) that mirrors safeParse exactly cut settings import 157ms->14ms and every command ~38% (--help 227->140ms, list 540->340ms). Lesson: profile per-module import cost (node --input-type=module timing each import) before assuming the bottleneck is your own code; a single eager dep on the hot path dominates.

value.author: codex-subagent-dogfood-2026-05-23
value.text:
Manual dogfood design sub-agent proposal (no code edits): after current changes, run this isolated temp-directory smoke to cover agent lifecycle + compactness without touching repo tracker data.

Commands:
cd <repo>
pnpm build
WORK=$(mktemp -d)
mkdir -p "$WORK/project"
printf "# manual dogfood\n" > "$WORK/project/README.md"
export PM_AUTHOR=codex-manual-dogfood-smoke-2026-05-23
export PM_PATH="$WORK/project/.agents/pm"
export PM_GLOBAL_PATH="$WORK/global"
PM="node <repo>/dist/cli.js"
$PM --json init --defaults --author "$PM_AUTHOR" --with-packages
$PM --json config project set test-result-tracking --policy enabled
$PM --json create --title "Dogfood dependency blocker" --description "Blocker used to verify dependency graph output." --type Task --status open --priority 2 --create-mode progressive
export BLOCKER_ID=<item.id from previous JSON>
$PM --json create --title "Dogfood compact lifecycle" --description "Manual smoke for create/update/test/files/docs/deps/search/plan/history compactness." --type Task --status open --priority 1 --tags dogfood,agent-ux,compact --acceptance-criteria "Lifecycle, linked evidence, deps, search, plan, and history compactness pass." --create-mode progressive --comment "created by isolated manual smoke"
export ROOT_ID=<item.id from previous JSON>
$PM --json update "$ROOT_ID" --status in_progress --estimate 20 --blocked-by "$BLOCKER_ID" --comment "start manual smoke and attach blocker"
$PM --json files "$ROOT_ID" --add "path=README.md,scope=project,note=temp project marker"
$PM --json docs "$ROOT_ID" --add "path=README.md,scope=project,note=temp docs marker"
$PM --json test "$ROOT_ID" --add "command=node -e \"console.log(\\\"linked smoke ok\\\")\",scope=project,timeout_seconds=30"
$PM --json test "$ROOT_ID" --run --progress --fail-on-skipped --fail-on-empty-test-run
$PM --json deps "$ROOT_ID" --format graph --summary
$PM --json deps "$ROOT_ID" --format tree --max-depth 2 --collapse repeated
$PM --json get "$ROOT_ID" --depth brief
$PM --json search "Dogfood compact lifecycle" --limit 5 --fields id,title,status,type
$PM --json search "Dogfood compact lifecycle" --include-linked --limit 5 --full
$PM --json history "$ROOT_ID" --limit 8
$PM --json history "$ROOT_ID" --full --limit 8
$PM --json history "$ROOT_ID" --verify
$PM --json plan create --title "Dogfood plan compactness" --scope "Manual Plan lifecycle smoke" --harness codex-manual --parent "$ROOT_ID"
export PLAN_ID=<plan.id from previous JSON>
$PM --json plan add-step "$PLAN_ID" --step-title "Exercise linked lifecycle" --step-body "Use linked files/docs/tests and deps"
$PM --json plan add-step "$PLAN_ID" --step-title "Verify compact surfaces" --depends-on "$ROOT_ID"
$PM --json plan update-step "$PLAN_ID" plan-step-001 --step-status in_progress --step-evidence "lifecycle commands started"
$PM --json plan complete-step "$PLAN_ID" plan-step-001 --step-evidence "linked lifecycle commands passed"
$PM --json plan decision "$PLAN_ID" --decision-text "Use compact defaults plus explicit full escape hatches" --decision-rationale "Agents need short loops and inspectable detail on demand"
$PM --json plan validation "$PLAN_ID" --validation-text "history verification remains ok" --validation-command "pm history <id> --verify"
$PM --json plan approve "$PLAN_ID"
$PM --json plan materialize "$PLAN_ID" --steps plan-step-002 --materialize-type Task --materialize-parent "$ROOT_ID"
$PM --json plan show "$PLAN_ID" --fields id,title,steps_summary
$PM --json history "$PLAN_ID" --verify
$PM --json close "$PLAN_ID" "plan manual smoke complete" --validate-close warn
$PM --json close "$BLOCKER_ID" "blocker graph smoke complete" --validate-close warn
$PM --json close "$ROOT_ID" "manual compact lifecycle smoke complete" --validate-close warn
$PM --json validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift
$PM --json health --check-only --no-refresh --brief

Why this covers agent workflows: it uses sandboxed PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH, exercises create/update plus linked files/docs/tests, proves blocked-by deps are visible as summary and tree, compares compact/default read paths against --full escapes for search/history, runs the Plan create/step/decision/validation/materialize loop, verifies history chains, and finishes with validate/health gates. Keep the full scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs harness for package/SDK/calendar coverage; this sequence is the short manual smoke for lifecycle and compactness regressions.

Commands run: npm install -g .; pm --version; node -v; pnpm -v; pnpm build; pm context --limit 10; pm search "security observability telemetry sentry private leakage dogfood pm-rnpb pm-xk39" --limit 10; pm list-in-progress --limit 20; pm list-open --limit 20; git diff --cached --stat; git diff --stat; git diff --cached --name-only; git diff --name-only; rg --files for sentry/telemetry/secret/dogfood gates; node scripts/check-secrets.mjs; git diff regex scan for credential/private-host patterns; find scripts telemetry/sentry helpers; git ls-files scripts/prod scripts/prod/telemetry; git status --short --ignored scripts/prod scripts/prod/telemetry; pm health --check-only --brief --check-telemetry --json; node scripts/release/sentry-telemetry-gate.mjs --json --telemetry-mode required --max-critical 10 --max-high 20 --max-telemetry-error-rate 10; bash scripts/prod/telemetry/check-stack-health.sh; pm history pm-rnpb --verify --json; pm history pm-xk39 --verify --json; node scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs --help (note: this script treats --help as normal execution and completed the default isolated dogfood run).

Findings: no staged changes are present. Unstaged changes are limited to pm tracker/history files plus scoped CLI/test files already in the worktree. Tracked-file secret scan passed with no credential-like secrets. Diff regex scan produced only pm evidence text mentioning generic security terms, not credential values. scripts/prod remains ignored and untracked, preserving the private telemetry-ops boundary. Observability scripts identified: scripts/check-secrets.mjs, scripts/release/sentry-telemetry-gate.mjs, scripts/release/run-gates.mjs, scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs, plus ignored private helpers under scripts/prod/telemetry.

Live health: pm health brief telemetry returned ok=true with telemetry enabled, redacted capture, queue_entries=3, queue_draining=true, queue_invalid_rows=0, recent successful flush, and endpoint probe 200. Dedicated Sentry/telemetry gate passed in required mode with Sentry critical=0 high=0 total=0 and telemetry finish_error_rate_pct=3.11 with missing-error-code rows=0. Private stack helper showed telemetry containers up, API healthy, queue ready=0 consumers=1, and 3614 events in the last 24h. History verification for pm-rnpb and pm-xk39 returned ok=true/current_matches_latest=true.

Manual dogfood recommendation for the integrated change: run an explicit isolated temp-project flow using repo-local dist after build, with PM_AUTHOR set and both PM_PATH and PM_GLOBAL_PATH under a fresh temp root; exercise init --defaults --with-packages, package list/catalog/doctor, create/update/claim/files/docs/comments/notes/learnings/test, search/context/health/validate, and close/release on a throwaway item. Then run PM_DOGFOOD_SEMANTIC=1 node scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs for semantic coverage and node scripts/release/run-gates.mjs --json --telemetry-mode required --max-sentry-critical 10 --max-sentry-high 20 --max-telemetry-error-rate 10 for final release parity. Baseline dogfood evidence from this pass: default scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs completed 84 workflows; semantic mode was not attempted because PM_DOGFOOD_SEMANTIC was not set.

Decision/risk: no tiny fix required in this pass. Current observable risk is not a fresh secret leak; it is the standing pm-xk39 class of history/privacy hygiene, which remains the right lineage for future redaction work if a real leaked value is found.

value.author: codex-dedupe-agent
value.text: PR evidence: opened PR #64 Deduplicate extension contracts and plugin docs at https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/pull/64 from refactor/extension-plugin-contract-dedupe commit cee9f9fa. Marked ready for review and starting required >=15 minute AI-review wait plus GitHub check watch before merge.

value.author: codex-dedupe-agent
value.text: Review feedback handling: Gemini flagged DEFAULT_EXTENSION_POLICY top-level mutability and structuredClone overhead. Accepted valid parts by changing createDefaultExtensionGovernancePolicy() to return a fresh literal object, freezing the loader's module-level DEFAULT_EXTENSION_POLICY fallback, and adding a regression that separate default policies have independent arrays. CodeRabbit's .agents/pm history warning is false positive for this repo because all tracker/history changes in this branch were created by pm CLI commands, not manual file edits.

value.author: codex-dedupe-agent
value.text: PR update evidence: pushed review-fix commit 4ce16f74 addressing Gemini extension policy feedback. Focused canonical extension contract/default regression passed with 74 tests. Added PR comment explaining the valid fix and why CodeRabbit's .agents/pm history warning is a false positive for pm-generated tracker history.
